content:
If you have your own server like Synology NAS or have access to other servers, you can write a sctipt in python to upload files to your remote server using python package called Paramiko.
I was trying to use function directly from Paramiko using SFTP (Secure File Transfer Protocol) client, but I getting error saying Channel is closed. So, I used the scp module from SCP. The code given below is what I used to upload my files to the server using python.
#!/Users/user_name/opt/anaconda3/envs/py37/bin/python import paramiko from scp import SCPClient #creating ssh client ssh_client = paramiko.SSHClient() #when you are logging to server, you can see a message to add key, and you have to select yes. # this is what the following code is doing ssh_client.set_missing_host_key_policy(paramiko.AutoAddPolicy()) #logging into the server ssh_client.connect(hostname = '192.168.0.1', port=22, username='user_name',password='password') scp_client = SCPClient(ssh.get_transport(), socket_timeout=10) # Uploading 'local_folder' directory and its content in the remote server to path /remote/path/ scp_client.put('local_folder', recursive=True, remote_path='/remote/path/local_folder') scp_client.close() ssh_client.close()  Automating the job This is fine if you want to do it only once, but if you are modiyfing files or generating new files daily, then you can automate the task to run the above script at certain time daily. I am using macbook, and you can do that using following steps :
In your terminal type : crontab -e
A file will open, then you can type: 0 21 * * * python /path/upload.py >> ~/cron.log 2>&1
and then save the file. Here 0 represents minutes, 21 represents hour, which is 9 pm. And since we want to run this command everyday for every other month, all other values are *. If you want to run certain number of days in a month you can do so by changing the value. You can click here for more details
 NOTE : If you are using separate environment to run the script, then you can add the shebang line on top of your code as: #!/Users/username/opt/anaconda3/envs/env_name/bin/python.
  You can use conda env list to get the path of your environment.
  If your cron job is not done, then check the log file, probably cron doesn’t have access to the script, if so then you need to provide access to cron. Here, is the link for that.
 Links   SCP
  Cron Job Scheduling Mac
  Cron permission access
  Job Scheduling Windows
description: Upload files daily at specific time to create backup
title: Automatically Upload Files to Remote Server Using Python

content:
This blog is a personal blog for note taking of things I learn and keeping track of things. This blog is mainly focussed in the field of Bioinformatics and Computational Biology.
The posts will mainly contain codes related to python and some R programming. It will also include some statistical concepts and recent papers published in the field. All other things that doesn’t fall with in these categories will be added to ‘Other_stuffs’.
Dewan Shrestha
dewshrs@gmail.com
